description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a wide fuselage, swept-back low-mounted wings, and four underwing turbofan engines, each with red nacelles. The aircraft features a distinctive hump on the forward upper fuselage, characteristic of a double-deck design, and a prominent vertical stabilizer with a red and white logo. The main body is painted white with dark windows and detailed livery, including the word 'WESTLINE' and additional markings along the side. The tail section includes a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it is either retracted or not modeled in detail. The overall design is streamlined for high-speed, long-distance flight, typical of wide-body passenger jets.
description: A large, white commercial jet airliner with four red underwing engines, a double-deck forward fuselage, and a prominent vertical tail fin with logo.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from an elevated front-left angle, highlighting the swept wings, four red engines, and the double-deck forward fuselage.
description_view_1: A direct top-down view shows the full wingspan, engine placement, and the livery details along the fuselage.
description_view_2: The underside rear view emphasizes the wing sweep, engine nacelles, and the tailplane configuration.
description_view_3: A side profile from the right displays the length of the fuselage, the upper deck hump, and the vertical stabilizer with its logo.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on aircraft type.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of a multi-seat commercial airliner.
